title: Scleroxylum melanophloeum
taxonomy: Scleroxylum melanophloeum is classified under the family Meliaceae, which is a family of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs commonly known as the mahogany family. This family is a part of the order Sapindales, which includes other flowering plants such as the soapberry and the lychee. The family Meliaceae is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, including Africa, Asia, and the Americas. Scleroxylum melanophloeum is a species of tree that is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Thailand and Myanmar. The classification of Scleroxylum melanophloeum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Meliaceae family.
uses: Scleroxylum melanophloeum is a plant species native to tropical Asia, and while it has various uses, information on its medicinal uses is limited. intro: Scleroxylum melanophloeum, also known as the Indian rose chestnut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its unique, rose-colored flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Scleroxylum melanophloeum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a tincture or paste, to treat a range of health conditions.
